topic: World History Topics (two, same as SL)
subject: history

items.q: Why did Stalin reject the Marshall Plan?
items.correct: He saw it as dollar imperialism that would pull Eastern Europe into the US orbit; he set up Comecon as an Eastern bloc alternative and blocked satellite states from joining.
items.misconception: Stalin rejected the Marshall Plan because the USSR did not need money after WWII.
items.random_wrong: Stalin rejected the Marshall Plan because it required countries to fight Germany again.
items.why: The USSR was devastated by war and desperately needed aid. Stalin's refusal was strategic, not economic — accepting US dollars meant accepting US influence.

items.correct: The release of the White House tapes proved he obstructed the FBI's Watergate inquiry; facing certain impeachment by Congress, he resigned to avoid conviction.
items.misconception: Nixon resigned because he personally ordered the Watergate break-in.
items.random_wrong: Nixon resigned because he lost the 1972 election to Carter.
items.why: Nixon's crime was the cover-up, not necessarily ordering the burglary. Students assume worse acts when the constitutional issue was actually obstruction of justice.

items.q: What caused the First World War?
items.correct: Long-term tensions (militarism, alliances, imperialism, nationalism) combined with the July Crisis triggered by the assassination of Franz Ferdinand.
items.misconception: The assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand caused the war.
items.random_wrong: Germany declared war on Britain to seize colonies.
items.why: The assassination was the trigger, not the cause. Students treat the spark as sufficient explanation, ignoring the systemic pressures (MAIN factors) that made a wider war possible.

items.q: Which 1938 conference ceded the Sudetenland to Germany?
items.why: Correct answer: Munich. “Locarno” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Who signed the 1939 non-aggression pact for the USSR?
items.why: It's Molotov. “Litvinov” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In what month and year did Hitler remilitarise the Rhineland?
items.why: Exact answer: 1936 AD. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
